steps: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of \(v^{4}\) and \(460v^{3}\). Both terms share \(v^{3}\).
That matches choice C — C is correct.

choices.elimination:
• Expand: \((v^{2}+46)(v^{2}-10) = v^{4} - 10v^{2} + 46v^{2} - 460 = v^{4} + 36v^{2} - 460\).
  • This is a degree-4 polynomial with a \(v^{2}\) term and a constant, neither of which appears in the original expression.
  • Eliminated because expansion does not equal \(v^{4} - 460v^{3}\).

• Expand: \((v^{2}-46)(v^{2}-10) = v^{4} - 10v^{2} - 46v^{2} + 460 = v^{4} - 56v^{2} + 460\).
  • Again this has a \(v^{2}\) term and a constant, which the original expression lacks.
  • Eliminated because expansion does not equal \(v^{4} - 460v^{3}\).

• Factor out \(v^{3}\): \(v^{4} - 460v^{3} = v^{3} \cdot v - v^{3} \cdot 460 = v^{3}(v - 460)\).
  • Expanding confirms: \(v^{3}(v-460) = v^{4} - 460v^{3}\).
  • That matches choice C — C is correct.

• Expanding: \(v^{3}(v+460) = v^{4} + 460v^{3}\).
  • The sign on the second term is positive, but the original has a minus sign: \(v^{4} - 460v^{3}\).
  • Eliminated because the sign is wrong.
